milestone: Scene-hosted Experiences are now resizable and repositionable
slice: Two related, user-reported concerns closed as one product change. (1) A Scene-hosted Experience's Text/Image/Graphics Transforms (X/Y/W/H) were read-only in the Inspector, with a self-heal that pinned them back to full bleed (0,0,1,1) on every render — the author literally could not resize a Scene-hosted Experience to anything but the full Scene. (2) There was no Experience mode that combined 'sits behind the picture frame' with 'author-sized and positioned' — Scene-hosted was permanently full-bleed, Free-hosted always rendered above the picture. Both concerns share one fix: Scene-hosted's semantic meaning shifts from 'always fills the scene (read-only)' to 'sits behind the picture frame at whatever size you want (full bleed by default).' The distinction from Free-hosted is now purely z-order — Scene-hosted always converges onto target:'slide' (behind Frame/Panel), Free-hosted stays on target:'overlay' (in front). Preserved as sensible defaults: new Scene-hosted Experiences seed their content Transforms to full-bleed on creation (defaultProperties/defaultUniversalContent forward the hostedBy value so this is a real data default, not just a UI trick), so the pre-change 'starts filling the Scene' UX is unchanged; and a one-click '↔ Fill Scene' quick-action lives under a Scene-hosted Experience's Transform to snap back to full bleed after resizing. Backward-compat preserved for a pre-hostedBy plain kind:'fill' background (Blueprint §9's own 'set the background') — still resolves to target:'slide' when full-bleed. Place-hosted stays read-only, deliberately and disclosed: a Place-hosted Experience maps to the Place's Frame slot (an enum reference, not a rect), so unlocking bounds there wouldn't render anything meaningful. tools/world-builder/ (v1) is untouched, per the v2-only scope of this round. Verified end-to-end via a new Playwright test driving the REAL ProjectCompiler.runBuild pipeline (not a mock of convergeSceneLayer) against a project containing every relevant Scene Layer shape — full-bleed Scene-hosted, resized-smaller Scene-hosted, Free-hosted, and a legacy kind:'fill' background — all four resolve to the correct compile target, zero console errors, goldenBuild.js's full 30-assertion suite passes unchanged.
